extraction.one_line_summary: BAUHAUS Phil won the sprint by switching to the opposite side of the road after finding no space, avoiding barriers that caught CAVENDISH Mark and KRISTOFF Alexander.
tactical_patterns: BAUHAUS switched road sides in final meters when boxed, exploiting sprinters' tunnel vision on sunny side positioning.
Israel Premier Tech led through technical section under 3km but failed converting NIZZOLO, suggesting leadout dissolution point.
Four-lap circuit allowed teams to preview sketchy corners around 800m marker, yet crashes still occurred final lap.
Breakaway never exceeded four minutes on sprinter-friendly stage, revealing teams' precise gap management to preserve sprint trains.

rider_signals.BAUHAUS Phil: positive: demonstrated tactical awareness to exploit road positioning when boxed, earning rare win against deeper sprint field
rider_signals.NIZZOLO Giacomo: negative: Israel Premier Tech delivered perfect leadout through technical zone but failed to convert positioning into result
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: caught in barriers after being squeezed by charging sprinters, suggesting positioning vulnerability in technical finishes
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: negative: involved in barrier incident despite stage profile suiting him, raising questions about form recovery after team crashes
rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: positive: podium finish without visible leadout support indicates emerging ability to self-position in chaotic sprints
rider_signals.DAINESE Alberto: positive: top-five result demonstrates improving sprint maturity in technical finale without prominent leadout
rider_signals.MERLIER Tim: negative: completely missed result despite being marquee sprinter, suggesting either positioning failure or form concerns
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: neutral: controlled race to secure overall and multiple jerseys with ACKERMANN Pascal support, revealing team sprint capacity
